Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: Web Audio Studio – A Visual Debugger for Web Audio API Graphs 42 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. First-ever in-utero stem cell therapy for fetal spina bifida repair is safe 72 upvotes, 6 comments
  3. Felix "fx" Lindner has died 29 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Neocaml – Rubocop Creator's New OCaml Mode for Emacs 73 upvotes, 12 comments
  5. Launch HN: OctaPulse (YC W26) – Robotics and computer vision for fish farming 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. An Interesting Find: STM32 RDP1 Decryptor 52 upvotes, 9 comments
  7. Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS Foundation 1457 upvotes, 503 comments
  8. AMD Am386 released March 2, 1991 61 upvotes, 13 comments
  9. Parallel coding agents with tmux and Markdown specs 29 upvotes, 5 comments
  10. Show HN: Omni – Open-source workplace search and chat, built on Postgres 117 upvotes, 35 comments
  11. Microsoft bans the word "Microslop" on its Discord, then locks the server 698 upvotes, 273 comments
  12. Inside the M4 Apple Neural Engine, Part 1: Reverse Engineering 122 upvotes, 39 comments
  13. Jolla phone – a full-stack European alternative 372 upvotes, 157 comments
  14. Why Go Can't Try 29 upvotes, 7 comments
  15. Packaging a Gleam app into a single executable 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. Making Video Games in 2025 (without an engine) 318 upvotes, 146 comments
  17. Anthropic Cowork feature creates 10GB VM bundle on macOS without warning 259 upvotes, 124 comments
  18. Use the Mikado Method to do safe changes in a complex codebase 64 upvotes, 24 comments
  19. /e/OS is a complete "deGoogled", mobile ecosystem 505 upvotes, 280 comments
  20. Mondrian Entered the Public Domain. The Estate Disagrees 146 upvotes, 74 comments
  21. A bit of fluid mechanics from scratch not from scratch 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  22. Zclaw – The 888 KiB Assistant 19 upvotes, 7 comments
  23. If AI writes code, should the session be part of the commit? 416 upvotes, 355 comments
  24. Judge finalizes order for Greenpeace to pay $345M in ND oil pipeline case 117 upvotes, 95 comments
  25. Notes on Lagrange Interpolating Polynomials 11 upvotes, 4 comments
  26. Why Objective-C 54 upvotes, 40 comments
  27. OpenClaw Surpasses React to Become the Most-Starred Software Project on GitHub 192 upvotes, 192 comments
  28. How to talk to anyone and why you should 370 upvotes, 430 comments
  29. Ask HN: Who is hiring? (March 2026) 52 upvotes, 70 comments

Last generated 1:30am, 3 March 2026. Source here.
